column13: Comments
release from recent snow and wind
release from recent snow and wind
Eagle & Telescope - debris mixed with mitigation debris.
Did not affect road.  Two small narrow slides originating from wind loaded upper rock bands
Did not affect highway.  Shallow -  narrow slide but ran almost full track - within 200 ft of road.
no vis for crown descriptions
50 ft from road

Stopped 500+ ft from road.  Debris shallow as most of the new snow was confined to upper wind-loaded slopes.  Began as shallow slab at top pushing down steep confined couloir through shallow new snow.  Mid-track it behaved more like a wet release (the sun had just popped out following a strong NW wind/snow event). Significance is how far it ran on the old slick surface.

Bullion King & Muleshoe .  We saw debris in these runouts before we performed mitigation -  but vis was very poor.  One debris pile looks fresher -  but difficult to say whether some newer snow on top of the debris settled making it easier to see today.
ran 1/4 track. Non road hits
cross loaded gully feature
Another small slide resulting from north winds loading over top of eagle/telescope ridge.  Initiated within rocks at top of path
Small release from top of path on lookers right side.  Resulted from north winds loading into starting zone.
Crowns were hard to pick out (very shallow wind triggered) - mostly saw the debris in track. Strip of snow running down the main gully. Not near enough volume to reach the road.
many of these crowns are filled in from additional snow/wind. narrow line of debris ran thru bottom cliffs stopping at top of apron

Feel strongly that wind triggered this slide. Attached photos show intense wind transport from N to S. The slide had not occurred when the photo was taken but was seen on the way back down from the pass. Too wind obscured to see the crown very well. Funnelled down a tight gully -  the shallow debris reached the top of the apron.
ran mid track
Reached road 3' x 300'
crown filled in - suggesting it ran during the storm.  Narrow debris pile came 200' from road
Natural while road was closed
Shallow crown -  but good propagation.  Ran narrow and only reached half-track.
Debris came 150' above rd.  Saw debris above road.  A witness later confirmed seeing it run at 8:30am
Came within 150' of hwy.
Road Closed - did not reach hwy
Crown re-covered by additional snow. Shallow debris ran reached lower apron. Not a road hit. Group of these ran during overnight high PI
Significant slide reached edge of road with small amount of debris.
ran to 400' of road
debris came out of cliff- couldn't tell how much. Didn't look like a lot.
ran to 200' of road
One of these ran this morning. The other - we watched release due to active heavy wind loading. Slide only involved new snow but ran with impressive speed.
This was a very similar avalanche to the Storm Slab avalanche that hit the road during the holiday storm. Guessing it is running on a crust and has been triggered by strong NW winds.
Shallow wind slab
Appears to have run yesterday - crown and bed surface have been covered or obliterated by the wind. Stopped a few hundred yards short of the highway.
Avalanche stayed on upper bench. Did not hit road.
Warm day.
Triggered by a period of high PI and a wind shift to the west.
Likely triggered by strong winds.
